---
title: "linear regression"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
jupyter: python3
---

$y_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x_i + \epsilon_i$ on crude oil.

```{python}
import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import datetime as dt
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score

ticker = "CL=F"
df = yf.download(ticker, start="2020-01-01", end="2026-03-15", progress=False)
df = df[['Close']].dropna().reset_index()

X = df['Date'].map(dt.datetime.toordinal).values.reshape(-1, 1)
y = df['Close'].values

model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(X, y)

r2 = r2_score(y, model.predict(X))

future_dates = pd.date_range(start="2026-03-16", end="2027-12-31", freq='D')
X_future = future_dates.map(dt.datetime.toordinal).values.reshape(-1, 1)
y_future = model.predict(X_future)

plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df['Date'], y, color='lightgray', s=10)
plt.plot(df['Date'], model.predict(X), color='red')
plt.plot(future_dates, y_future, color='green', linestyle='--')

plt.show()
```
